subject: English
title: The Happy Prince – Medium Short Notes

key_points: The prince lived a life of luxury and ignorance before becoming a statue
From the statue he observes the suffering of poor people
The swallow helps him distribute his ruby, sapphires, and gold leaves
The prince sacrifices all his wealth to help others
The swallow sacrifices his journey to Egypt and eventually his life
characters: The Happy Prince – Represents compassion and sacrifice
The Swallow – Symbol of loyalty and friendship
The Mayor and Councillors – Represent superficial judgment
themes: Social inequality between rich and poor
literary_devices: Irony – Society values the statue only when it looks beautiful
Symbolism – The lead heart represents the prince’s compassion
Contrast – Rich people’s luxury vs poor people’s suffering
exam_tips: Explain the transformation of the prince after death
Discuss the swallow’s role as a messenger
Mention sacrifice as the central theme
Use examples of poor characters helped by the prince
